items.question: Which workflow is simplest for a SaaS startup deploying multiple times a day?
Which branch in GitFlow always reflects the current production code?
items.explanation: `develop` is the integration branch; `main` is what's in production.

items.question: Trunk-based development relies heavily on:
items.explanation: Flags hide unfinished work; CI keeps `main` deployable.

items.question: An annotated tag differs from a lightweight tag because:
options: it carries author, date, message and optional signature
items.answer: it carries author, date, message and optional signature
items.explanation: Annotated tags are full Git objects with metadata.

items.question: After landing a GitFlow hotfix on `main`, what MUST you do?
options: merge it back into `develop` so the fix isn't lost in the next release
items.answer: merge it back into `develop` so the fix isn't lost in the next release
items.explanation: Otherwise the next release branch (cut from `develop`) silently regresses.

items.question: Which is the most common anti-pattern in workflow choice?
options: GitFlow at a small startup with one supported version
items.answer: GitFlow at a small startup with one supported version
items.explanation: GitFlow's ceremony only pays off when you have parallel supported versions.
